my_thoughts: The validation/exit works: vicious circle = correct (aporia), system = only exit (mechanism for escape). The student recognized despair (intellectual despair), which shows they understand: vicious circle = caught (despair). They'll likely accept the validation/exit. The episode is complete: causal test → warning/prediction → despair → validation/exit. Pattern complete.
accumulated_insight: Two-turn pattern: T1: Student tested causal efficacy (testing causal efficacy)—warning/prediction: self-deception, same result despite change. T2: Student recognized despair ('It seems that one is caught up in a vicious circle!')—intellectual despair via conceptual integration and despair. They understand: vicious circle = caught (despair). The ontological_shift mechanism validates: vicious circle = correct (aporia), system = only exit (mechanism for escape). Pattern complete: causal test → warning/prediction → despair recognition → validation/exit. They've shifted: vicious circle = recognized, system = exit (validation/exit).
visible_response:
Certainly one is caught, and the system is the way out of this vicious circle, for in
ordinary 
conditions of life one cannot get out.
